title: Polish Rice Pilaf
description: A sweet and pillowy Polish dish featuring rice, perfect for a brunch mood, made with rice paper sheets and steamed to perfection in an iron teppan, and is keto friendly.
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine mesh strainer until the water runs clear.
In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil.
Add the rice to the saucepan, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
While the rice cooks, cut the rice paper sheets into small pieces and set aside.
In a small skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the granulated sweetener, salt, and thyme to the skillet and stir until the sweetener dissolves.
Once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the sweetener mixture.
To steam the rice, place the iron teppan over medium heat, add 1 tablespoon of water, and bring to a simmer.
Add the cooked rice to the teppan, cover with a lid, and steam for 5 minutes.
Stir in the cut rice paper sheets and serve hot.
